fresh_facts.claim: The IDF conducted extensive strikes across Iran during the night of March 15-16, 2026, targeting regime infrastructure and nuclear-related facilities in Tehran.
More than 200 targets were struck in western and central Iran on March 15, 2026, including IRGC headquarters and Basij unit facilities in the Hamadan area.
The Supreme Leader's aircraft at Mehrabad Airport in Tehran was destroyed in Israeli strikes on March 15, 2026.
Iranian drone attacks on the United Arab Emirates declined from 332 on the second day of the war to six by March 16, 2026.
On March 10, 2026, a senior IRGC commander stated that Iran was shifting to firing only missiles with payloads of 1,000 kg or more.
Hezbollah activity levels peaked at 56 attack waves on March 14, 2026, then decreased to 34 waves on March 15, but remained sustained at dozens of attack waves per day.
US-led strikes on Iranian-backed Iraqi militia bases on March 11-12, 2026 killed 20 PMF fighters in al Qaim and targeted multiple bases across Iraq.
The PMF reported that the US has carried out 32 airstrikes targeting PMF bases across six Iraqi provinces since the war began, killing 27 members and injuring 50 others.
Israel's Operation Roaring Lion has struck 500 military targets using approximately 200 fighter jets, described as the largest combat sortie in Israeli history.
The campaign has degraded roughly 200 Iranian air defense systems and established effective control of airspace from western Iran to central Tehran within 24 hours.
As of mid-March 2026, 13 US military members have been killed in the campaign against Iran.

Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died and his son Mojtaba Khamenei was elevated to supreme leader by the Assembly of Experts, marking the first father-to-son transfer of power since Iran's 1979 revolution.
According to U.S. intelligence assessments cited by Reuters, the Iranian government is not yet at risk of collapse and the country's leadership structure remains largely intact.
Two senior Iranian intelligence figures—Abdollah Jalali-Nasab and Amir Shariat—were killed in Tehran strikes on March 15, 2026, with Iranian state media confirming Jalali-Nasab's death.
fresh_facts.source: Israeli military sources and Iranian state media

fresh_facts.claim: The UN Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on Iran formally warned on March 11, 2026 that Iranian civilians face escalating risks from simultaneous external military force and internal security measures.
fresh_facts.source: UN Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on Iran

fresh_facts.claim: Residents in Tehran reported anti-IRGC night chants, including 'death to the IRGC,' in the Saadat Abad district even as the regime expanded patrols and stop-and-search operations.
The regime's judiciary is reportedly preparing to implement methods associated with the 1988 massacre, signaling severe crackdowns on dissent.

The PMOI Resistance Units launched widespread grassroots campaigns across Iran defying heavy security, explicitly endorsing the National Council of Resistance of Iran's newly announced Provisional Government.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi adopted a defensive diplomatic posture on March 16, 2026, denying Iran targeted civilian areas in neighboring states.
Hamas broke from Tehran's wartime position, urging the Iranian regime not to target neighboring Arab countries while affirming Iran's right to self-defense.
On March 13, 2026, Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama announced plans to invite parliament to vote on a resolution declaring the 'Khomeinist Republic of Iran' a state sponsor of terrorism.
According to an IRGC Intelligence Organization statement released March 12, 2026, the agency claimed enemies are attempting to create fear and encourage street protests to produce instability.
On March 15, 2026, Iran entered its sixteenth day of war with military operations pressing into urban centers.
Israeli military operations on March 16 included strikes across multiple major Iranian cities including Tabriz and Shiraz.
A base of the Iranian army's air force in Isfahan was attacked during Israeli strikes on March 15, 2026.
According to military sources, more than one hundred targets connected to the regime's production systems have been struck in recent days as of mid-March 2026.
The IDF expanded ground operations in southern Lebanon, focusing on key targets including Radwan Force headquarters and launch sites to expand defensive space.

regime_fall.current_status: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has died and been succeeded by his son Mojtaba Khamenei in the first father-to-son transfer of power since 1979, while US-Israeli forces continue intensive military operations that have degraded Iranian air defenses and struck over 500 targets across the country, though US intelligence assessments indicate the regime's leadership structure remains largely intact despite widespread internal dissent and security crackdowns.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died and was succeeded by his son Mojtaba Khamenei on March 16, 2026, marking a historic succession within Iran's revolutionary system amid unprecedented military pressure and internal instability.
